title: Wind turbine & method for installing a wind turbine
abstract: A wind turbine for deployment offshore. The wind turbine including: a tower-float assembly having a tower (3) for supporting a nacelle (13a) and a rotor (13b), and a float (5) arranged to maintain at least part of the tower above a surface of a body of water; a keel assembly (7) including at least one keel module (25) and at least one rod (9) connecting the keel module to the tower-float assembly, wherein the at least one rod is arranged to move relative to the tower-float assembly to deploy the keel module, and the keel module is movable relative to the tower-float assembly, in response to movement of the at least one rod, between a non-deployed position proximal the tower-float assembly and a deployed position which is distal from the tower-float assembly in a downwardly direction, thereby increasing an effective length of the wind turbine; and the at least one rod is arranged to transfer bending moments to the tower-float assembly.
claims: Claims 1. A wind turbine for deployment offshore, including: a tower-float assembly having a tower for supporting a nacelle and a rotor, and a float arranged to maintain at least part of the tower above a surface of a body of water; a keel assembly including at least one keel module and at least one rod connecting the keel module to the tower-float assembly, wherein the at least one rod is arranged to move relative to the tower-float assembly to deploy the keel module, and the keel module is movable relative to the tower-float assembly, in response to movement of the at least one rod, between a non-deployed position proximal the tower-float assembly and a deployed position which is distal from the tower-float assembly in a downwardly direction, thereby increasing an effective length of the wind turbine; and the at least one rod is arranged to transfer bending moments between the keel module and the tower-float assembly in a deployed condition. 2. A wind turbine according to claim 1, wherein the at least one rod is arranged to transmit at least one of compressive forces and shear forces between the keel module and the tower-float assembly in the deployed condition. 3. A wind turbine according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the at least one rod is arranged to move translationally relative to the tower-float assembly along a rectilinear path, and the keel module is moveable from the non-deployed position to the deployed position along a rectilinear path. 4. A wind turbine according to claim 3, wherein the rectilinear path is a generally vertical path. 5. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one rod is pivotally attached to the tower-float assembly and is arranged for pivoting movement relative to the tower-float assembly, and the keel module is moveable from the non-deployed position to the deployed position along a curved path. 6. A wind turbine according to claim 5, wherein the at least one rod is arranged to pivot through an angle of approximately 90 degrees from a non-deployed condition to the deployed condition. 7. A wind turbine according to claim 5 or 6, wherein the at least one rod is arranged generally horizontally when the keel module is in the non-deployed position and the at least one rod is arranged generally vertically when the keel module is in the deployed position. 8. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, including a plurality of rods connecting the keel module to tower-float assembly. 9. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, including a plurality of keel modules. 10. A wind turbine according to claim 9 when dependent on claim 5, wherein the at least one rod comprises a first rod connecting the keel member to the tower-float assembly, the first rod is pivotally attached to the tower float assembly at a first pivot axis; and including a second rod connecting a second keel member to the tower-float assembly, the second rod is pivotally attached to the tower float assembly at a second pivot axis, wherein the second rod is arranged to overlap the first rod when the keel module and second keel module are each in their respective non-deployed positions. 11. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, including a drive system having at least one drive unit arranged to move the keel module from the non-deployed position to the deployed position. 12. A wind turbine according to claim 11, wherein the at least one drive unit is releasably attachable to the tower-float assembly and is removable from the tower-float assembly after the keel is moved to the deployed position. 13. A wind turbine according to claim 11 or 12, wherein the drive system includes a plurality of drive units. 14. A wind turbine according to claim 13, wherein the drive system includes at least one drive unit per keel module; and/or the drive system includes at least one drive unit per rod. 15. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 11 to 14, including a controller for synchronising operation of each drive unit. 16. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 11 to 15, wherein at least one drive unit comprises a hydraulic drive unit; and/or the at least one drive unit comprises an electric motor. 17. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 11 to 16, wherein at least one, and preferably each, drive unit comprises a strand jack. 18. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 11 to 17, including a drive mechanism for transmitting drive from the drive unit to the at least one rod, wherein at least one rod includes drive formations and the drive mechanism includes at least one drive device arranged to selectively engage the drive formations to selectively transmit drive to at least one rod. 19. A wind turbine according to claim 18, wherein a plurality of rods include drive formations and the drive mechanism includes a plurality of drive devices arranged to selectively engage drive formations on respective rods. 20. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 17 to 19, wherein the drive mechanism includes a rack and pinion system. 21. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one rod is tubular. 22. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, including a plurality of rods connected together by bracing members; including a plurality of rods connected together by an annular member. 23. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, including a plurality of guides for guiding movement of the at least one rod relative to the tower-float assembly. 24. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one keel module includes a housing. 25. A wind turbine according to claim 24, wherein the housing has a hollow interior and the hollow interior is arranged to be filled with ballast. 26. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one keel module is connected to another keel module by a linkage. 27. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the keel modules are arranged in a plane. 28. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the float includes a first set of buoyancy aids, each buoyancy aid has a respective keel module associated therewith, the respective keel module is movably connected to its respective buoyancy aid by at least one respective rod. 29. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the float includes a first buoyancy tank, the at least one rod comprises a first rod connecting the keel module to the first buoyancy tank, wherein the first rod is arranged to move relative to the first buoyancy tank to deploy the keel module from the non-deployed position to a deployed position; the float includes a second buoyancy tank, and including a second rod connecting a second keel module to the second buoyancy tank, wherein the second rod is arranged to move relative to the second buoyancy tank to deploy the second keel member from a non-deployed position to a deployed position. 30. A wind turbine according to claim 29, wherein the float includes a third buoyancy tank, and including a third rod connecting a third keel module to the third buoyancy tank, wherein the third rod is arranged to move relative to the third buoyancy tank to deploy the third keel member from a non-deployed position to a deployed position. 31. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 28 to 30 when dependent on claim 11, wherein the drive system is arranged to move each respective keel module with respect to its respective buoyancy tank. 32. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the float includes a second set of buoyancy aids, such as a set of buoyancy collars, that are removable from the float during an installation process. 33. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 28 to 30, wherein a longitudinal axis of the at least one rod is inclined to a longitudinal axis of a respective buoyancy tank when the keel member is in the deployed position; and/or wherein a longitudinal axis of the at least one rod is arranged inclined to a longitudinal axis of a respective buoyancy tank when the keel member is in the non-deployed position. 34. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 28 to 30, wherein a longitudinal axis of the at least one rod is arranged parallel with a longitudinal axis of a respective buoyancy tank when the keel member is in the deployed position; and/or wherein a longitudinal axis of the at least one rod is arranged parallel with a longitudinal axis of a respective buoyancy tank when the keel member is in the non-deployed position. 35. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a longitudinal axis of the at least rod is inclined to a longitudinal axis of the tower when the keel member is in the deployed position; and/or a longitudinal axis of the at least rod is inclined to a longitudinal axis of the tower when the keel member is in the non-deployed position. 36. A wind turbine according to any one of claims 1 to 34, wherein a longitudinal axis of the at least rod is parallel with a longitudinal axis of the tower when the keel member is in the deployed position; and/or a longitudinal axis of the at least rod is parallel with a longitudinal axis of the tower when the keel member is in the non-deployed position. 37. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one rod has a length that is greater than or equal to 30m. 38. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein there is a rigid connection between the at least one rod and the keel module. 39. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one rod has a fixed length. 40. A wind turbine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one rod includes first and second tubular members arranged concentrically.
